steps.title: Question type
steps.content: Research notes — the goal is to use a quotation to emphasize a potential problem with the wood-wide web theory. The only critical quotation in the notes is Nair and colleagues' concern that recent CMN claims are "disconnected from evidence."

steps.title: Reasoning
steps.content: To accomplish the goal, the choice must (a) quote and (b) point to a problem. The phrase “disconnected from evidence” highlights the gap between popular claims and supporting evidence.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• "Describing trees as "super-cooperators," Marsh first suggested that trees can exchange resources with one another in 1997.": the quoted phrase is positive and supports the theory rather than emphasizing a problem. ✗
  • "In "The Decay of the Wood-Wide Web?," Nair and colleagues note that common mycorrhizal networks "have captured the interest of broad audiences.": quotes neutral language about popularity, not the critical concern; does not emphasize a problem. ✗
  • "After evaluating dozens of CMN studies, Nair and colleagues expressed concern that recent claims about common mycorrhizal networks are "disconnected from evidence.": uses the critical quotation to highlight the problem — that claims outrun the evidence. ✓
  • "Despite the concerns expressed in the 2022 study "The Decay of the Wood-Wide Web?," the wood-wide web theory remains popular.": contains no quotation and therefore cannot accomplish the stated goal. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
